rationale: Add a procedural policy to avoid long CLI arguments by preferring file-based payloads and setting conservative inline limits. This reduces Windows arg-length risk without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.arg_length_guard.v1",
  "max_inline_chars": 8000,
  "max_inline_bytes": 12000,
  "prefer_file_payload": true,
  "file_payload_dir": "memory/procedural",
  "notes": "When composing CLI calls, write large JSON/text to a temp file and pass the file path instead of inline args. Use conservative thresholds to avoid Windows CreateProcess limits."
}

risks: Policy may be ignored by components that do not consult this file; no runtime effect until adopted.
expected_benefit: Provides a documented, low-risk guardrail for avoiding Windows CLI arg-length failures and encourages file-based payload usage.
